content.problemStatement:
Write a C program to reverse a given string using pointers.


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains a single string SSS - The string which needs to be reversed.


Output Format

Output on a single line, the reversed string.
constraints: S contains only lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single string S consisting of only lowercase English letters, the string to be reversed.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the reversed string on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: A single‑character string remains unchanged when reversed.

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases. For each string, use the two‑pointer technique: place one pointer at the start and another at the end of the character array and swap the characters while moving the pointers towards each other. This reverses the string in‑place in O(|S|) time and O(1) extra space. Append each result to the output buffer and print at the end.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total length of all strings) – each character is visited at most once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space per test case (ignoring input storage).
